compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context / Problem

`reconcile_check.py` is the last core module that compares local state against the vendor
snapshot in vendor shape: it lazy-loads `adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py` and
`adapters/jira/adf.py` by path (`reconcile_check.py:61-75`) and calls
`_assignee_matches(str(local_val), jira_val)` on the raw Jira assignee dict
(`reconcile_check.py:236`), `_extract_jira_field(jira_issue, "description")` +
`adf.fit_text_to_adf_limit` for ADF description normalization (`:241-242`), and
`_extract_jira_field(jira_issue, field)` for the nested status/priority `{"name": ...}`
unwrap (`:247`). The neutrality gate documents this as deliberately out of its scope
(`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py:94-99`). The epic bbf1 container
review flagged it as an unrecorded second vendor reach-through — epic AC4 admits only the
`inbound_fields.py` exemption, so reconcile-check must be canonicalized.

## Approach

Apply the epic's canonical-comparison principle, reusing the machinery the prerequisite
canonical-differ story establishes — no new port surface:

- Canonicalize the snapshot entry once per bound pair with the configured backend's
  `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local` (resolved via `select_backend(load_config())`, the
  seam proven at `outbound_differ.py:450-454`), and compare in local vocabulary:
  - assignee: neutral membership matching against the canonical `assignee_identity`
    dict `{display, email, account_id}` [rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f], replacing the
    `_assignee_matches` call at `reconcile_check.py:236`;
  - description: compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`)
    against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance, replacing the
    `_extract_jira_field`/`fit_text_to_adf_limit` pair at `reconcile_check.py:241-242`
    (mirroring the differ's description parity rule [rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f]);
  - status/priority: compare the local value against the canonically mapped remote value,
    replacing `_extract_jira_field` + `_values_match_with_mapping` at
    `reconcile_check.py:247-248`.
- The reported `jira_value` in each discrepancy record stays the RAW snapshot value
  (`reconcile_check.py:255-258`) — an opaque vendor payload passed through untouched, which
  the principle allows; report shape and consumers are unchanged.
- Delete the `_load_outbound_fields`/`_load_adf` sibling loads (`reconcile_check.py:61-75`);
  remove the gate's documented reconcile_check carve-out and extend the AST/lazy-load case in
  `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py` to cover `reconcile_check.py`.

Rejected alternative: leaving reconcile_check as a second recorded exemption — unlike
`inbound_fields.py` (which IS the Jira mapper implementation, kept at root only for loader
location-pinning), reconcile_check is genuinely neutral core logic (a checker that must work
for any backend), so exempting it would leave a real second-backend correctness hole: its
discrepancy scan would mis-flag every binding on a non-Jira backend, exactly the
runny-lens-strafe failure mode it was fixed for (`reconcile_check.py:217-224`).

## Scope

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile_check.py (canonical comparison; drop vendor sibling loads)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (remove the carve-out; cover reconcile_check.py)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (reconcile-check comparison behavior tests)

## Testing

- Happy path: a fully-in-sync bound pair yields zero discrepancies on a live-shaped snapshot
  (nested status/priority objects, dict assignee, ADF description) — the runny-lens-strafe
  regression case, now via canonical mapping.
- Edge/failure: assignee matching across accountId / email / displayName local values and
  both-empty; an oversized ADF-fitted description does not read as drift; a genuinely
  divergent field is reported with the RAW vendor `jira_value` (report shape unchanged);
  unmapped remote status compares as divergent.
- Gate: the extended neutrality case fails if `reconcile_check.py` re-grows an
  `adapters.jira` import or by-path vendor sibling load.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `reconcile_check.py` contains NO `adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` import (any nesting
      depth) and NO by-path load of a vendor sibling (`adapters/jira/...` path literal) —
      verified by the extended case in
      `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`, whose reconcile_check
      carve-out comment is removed.
- [ ] Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira across the Testing cases
      (including the runny-lens-strafe live-shape regression) and the report's `jira_value`
      stays the raw snapshot value — verified by the behavior tests in Testing.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; whole test tree collects with 0 errors.

## Plan-review remediation (drift re-review, G1G2 findings)

Three corrections, reusing 625b's now-merged canonical machinery verbatim:

1. **AC1 gate mechanism (by-path load detection).** The existing full-tree AST walker
   (`_all_imported_modules` / `test_scalar_core_imports_no_jira_adapter`) only detects
   `import`/`from ... import` nodes — it CANNOT catch reconcile_check's by-path load
   `_load_sibling("outbound_fields", "adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py")` (a string-literal
   path). So AC1 is enforced by TWO cases in `test_backend_neutrality.py`: (a) add
   `reconcile_check.py` to the `_ZERO_ADAPTER_IMPORT_SCALAR_CORE` AST-import tuple (catches any
   `adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` import at any depth), AND (b) a NEW deterministic
   source-text case asserting `reconcile_check.py` contains NO `"adapters/jira/"` path-literal
   string (catches the `_load_sibling(..., "adapters/jira/...")` by-path loads). Both go green
   only after the `_load_outbound_fields`/`_load_adf` sibling loads are deleted.

2. **Description comparison reuses 625b's parity EXACTLY — NOT `sanitize_description`.**
   `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` is the SEND-path sanitizer: it fits AND emits a
   `logger.warning` on truncation, which is wrong for a read-only diagnostic. Instead, mirror
   the canonical differ (`outbound_field_diff.py:270-279`): fit the local description via the
   injected `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_desc}, ticket=ticket)`
   `.get("description")` (ADF-fit through the port, NO warning), and compare with rstrip
   tolerance (the differ's `_text_matches`) against the mapper-decoded `canonical_remote
   ["description"]`. reconcile_check therefore resolves BOTH `backend.inbound` (canonicalize the
   snapshot) AND `backend.outbound` (description ADF-fit + `resolve_assignee` for the assignee
   identity) via `select_backend(load_config())`. An injection seam (optional `inbound_mapper`
   / `outbound_mapper` params defaulting to the lazy `select_backend` resolve, mirroring
   outbound_differ / outbound_comments) keeps the spec-loaded unit tests working.

3. **AC2 scope: the annotation-label case is an intended CORRECTNESS improvement (B3).** For a
   ticket carrying a `rebar-status:blocked` / `rebar-status:cancelled` annotation label,
   `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local` applies the annotation-label -> local-status precedence
   (inbound_fields), so the canonical comparison yields the local status the inbound differ
   actually dispatches — where the pre-ad44 RAW comparison (reading only the raw Jira workflow
   status) mis-flagged it. AC2's "identical decisions" is therefore scoped to exclude this one
   annotation-label case, which is recorded as an INTENDED improvement (reconcile-check now
   mirrors the real differ), not a regression. All other Testing cases remain byte-identical.

**Added acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] The AC1 neutrality gate uses BOTH the AST-import case (reconcile_check.py in the tuple)
      AND a source-text case asserting NO `"adapters/jira/"` path literal in reconcile_check.py
      — the reconcile_check carve-out comment in test_backend_neutrality.py is removed.
- [ ] The description comparison uses `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` (no warning) + rstrip
      `_text_matches` against the canonical remote description — NOT `sanitize_description` —
      verified by a behavior test; no `logger.warning` is emitted on a normal reconcile-check pass.
- [ ] A ticket with a `rebar-status:blocked`/`cancelled` annotation label reconciles to the
      correct local status (annotation-label precedence via the canonical mapper) — verified by
      a behavior test documenting this as the one intended difference from the raw comparison.

file_impact.reason: canonical comparison via InboundMapper; drop vendor sibling loads

compiled_state.title: Canonicalize reconcile_check's vendor-shape comparison (reuse the canonical differ machinery)
